SEARCH
Package com.icarus.events

Class OrganizerCreateEventTest

java.lang.Object
com.icarus.events.OrganizerCreateEventTest
public class OrganizerCreateEventTest extends Object
Instrumented UI tests for OrganizerCreateEventActivity.

User Stories Tested: US 02.01.04 As an organizer, I want to set a registration period. US 02.03.01 As an organizer I want to OPTIONALLY limit the number of entrants who can join my waiting list. US 02.02.03 As an organizer I want to enable or disable the geolocation requirement for my event.

Tests use temporary Firestore collections to avoid interfering with production event data.

Author:
Kito Lee Son

    Description

    Cleans up Firestore test data after each test.

  • Constructor Details

    • OrganizerCreateEventTest

      public OrganizerCreateEventTest()

  • Method Details

    • setup

      public void setup()
      Prepares the Firestore environment before each test.

      This method redirects the application to use a temporary events collection so test data does not affect the production database.

    • testRegistrationPeriodSaved

      public void testRegistrationPeriodSaved() throws InterruptedException
      Tests that an organizer can create an event with a registration start and end date.

      The test simulates an organizer filling out the event creation form, selecting registration start and end dates using date picker dialogs, and creating the event. After creation, the test verifies that the correct registration period was stored in Firestore.

      User Story Tested: US 02.01.04 As an organizer, I want to set a registration period.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore retrieval wait is interrupted

    • testWaitingListLimitSaved

      public void testWaitingListLimitSaved() throws InterruptedException
      Tests that an organizer can optionally set a waiting list limit when creating an event.

      The test simulates an organizer filling out the event creation form and specifying a maximum number of entrants allowed on the waiting list. After the event is created, the test verifies that the waiting list limit was correctly stored in Firestore.

      User Story Tested: US 02.03.01 As an organizer I want to OPTIONALLY limit the number of entrants who can join my waiting list.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore retrieval wait is interrupted

    • testGeolocationEnabled

      public void testGeolocationEnabled() throws InterruptedException
      Tests that an organizer can enable the geolocation requirement when creating an event.

      The test fills out the event creation form, enables the geolocation toggle, creates the event, and verifies that the geolocation requirement is stored as true in Firestore.

      User Story Tested: US 02.02.03 As an organizer I want to enable the geolocation requirement for my event.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException

    • testGeolocationDisabled

      public void testGeolocationDisabled() throws InterruptedException
      Tests that an organizer can disable the geolocation requirement when creating an event.

      The test fills out the event creation form without enabling the geolocation toggle and verifies that the geolocation requirement is stored as false in Firestore.

      User Story Tested: US 02.02.03 As an organizer I want to disable the geolocation requirement for my event.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException

    • cleanup

      public void cleanup() throws InterruptedException
      Cleans up Firestore test data after each test.

      This method removes any events created during testing and restores the default Firestore events collection.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the cleanup wait is interrupted
